---
title: ""
page-layout: article
---

::: {.hero}
# Hema Balarama

Economics PhD candidate at LSE studying inequality, gender and labor markets, and climate vulnerability in LMICs.

::: {.badge-row}
<span class="badge">inequality</span>
<span class="badge">labor markets</span>
<span class="badge">social norms</span>
<span class="badge">climate shocks</span>
:::

::: {.quick-links}
[Email](mailto:h.balarama@lse.ac.uk) | CV available on request
:::
:::

## Research Focus

::: {.grid-two}
::: {.focus-card}
### Mobility and inequality
How institutions and social norms shape intergenerational mobility and life chances.
:::

::: {.focus-card}
### Gender and labor
How women respond to labor demand, constraints, and policy interventions.
:::

::: {.focus-card}
### Climate vulnerability
How climate shocks affect household decisions, employment, and resilience.
:::

::: {.focus-card}
### Evidence and policy
Applied micro methods to produce policy-relevant evidence in LMIC settings.
:::
:::

## Site Map

- Research: project summaries, drafts, and talks
- Teaching: courses, tutorials, and materials
- Writing: public notes and essays
